methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Inversion Space

private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Cuby Technologies is a Series A-stage construction-technology company that has raised $23.4M to date. The Housing & Construction sector peer set consists of four private companies (ICON, Inversion Space, Cobod, Material Evolution) and no public comparables, limiting the applicability of traditional peer-median methodologies.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $23M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $47M. This method is grounded in observed venture funding patterns at the Series A stage but carries inherent stage-transition assumptions. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Housing & Construction peer universe—only one same-sector company exists in available data, falling short of the minimum three-point threshold required for reliable median estimation.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $40M to $54M, with a median point estimate of $47M. Key uncertainties include the absence of public market anchors for the sector, the limited depth of private peer data at equivalent stages, and reliance on a single methodological pillar. The range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression point estimate. Refinement would require either expanded Housing & Construction peer financials at Series A or clarity on capital deployment trajectory post-Series A.
